other_observers: Nancy Gobris and Susan Craig, master banders both; The banders found it in a mist net.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, RMBO Banding Station
description: A vireo (see bill photo) was captured at the RMBO Banding Station at Chico Basin Ranch. It had a gray head the contrasted with an olive back. A dark gray eyeline was darkest in the loral area and it extented (less dark) behind the eye. The chin and throat were lemon yellow and the breast was cream-colored. Above and below the eye was a small pocket of white feathers contracting with the cronw and the darker auriculars. Some of the features were affected by the bird being captured and hand-held, so the crest was slightly erected and the true shape (round) of the head was not apparent. The bird had what I would call a very long primary projection (photo). Although Sibley says the ends of the flight feathers are darker than the rest of the wingtips, the photograph does not show this aspect.Behaviors: In the hand when I saw it.Call: none heardPlumage: after second year (adult)
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
